title: Top 5 Geopolitics Headlines

slides.headline: Record-breaking solo row to Hawaii
slides.explanation: Kelsey Pfendler just became the first American woman to row solo from California to Hawaii. She was greeted by huge crowds in Honolulu after completing her incredible journey.

slides.headline: Australia warns doctors on AI tools
slides.explanation: Australian health officials are worried about privacy as doctors start using AI to transcribe patient visits. The government is now keeping a close watch on how these tools are being used.

slides.headline: Pickle company quits DC fair
slides.explanation: Mt Olive Pickle Company pulled out of a Washington DC fair this week. They left after seeing a Confederate flag displayed at a state booth, citing their commitment to human dignity.

slides.headline: Massive crowds at funeral in Iran
slides.explanation: Millions are expected in Tehran for the funeral of former Iranian leader Ali Khamenei. Mourners have gathered at the Grand Mosalla mosque following his death.

slides.headline: Ukraine strikes Russian oil site
slides.explanation: Ukraine has targeted a major oil terminal in St Petersburg. President Zelensky says the attack aims to cut off a key source of funding for the war.

caption: From a historic solo journey across the Pacific to major updates on the war in Ukraine and the funeral of Iran's former leader, here are today's top stories. Stay informed with Headlinne. Download our app for more. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: Midjourney fights back in court
slides.explanation: Midjourney is asking the court to make Disney, Warner Bros, and Universal share how they use AI. This follows a lawsuit those studios filed against the AI company.

slides.headline: Google's new AI ad sparks debate
slides.explanation: A new Google commercial imagines what the Declaration of Independence might look like if the Founding Fathers used AI tools. It is a creative look at how history and technology intersect.

slides.headline: Fanfiction writers clash over AI
slides.explanation: A new movement in the fanfiction community is trying to ban authors who use AI tools. However, the methods they are using are causing confusion and might unfairly target innocent writers.

slides.headline: Meet the AI rival aiming for open source
slides.explanation: Mistral AI is gaining attention as a competitor to OpenAI. Since launching in 2023, they have raised a lot of money with the goal of making advanced AI available to everyone.

slides.headline: Can your watch predict illness?
slides.explanation: Smartwatches are getting better at spotting changes in your body's patterns. These small changes might help you realize when it is time to check in with your doctor.

caption: From Hollywood lawsuits to the future of fanfiction, AI is making waves everywhere this week. Check out the top tech stories you need to know today. Swipe through for the full breakdown. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
